sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Angela, presented with a history of recurrent kidney stones, with this being her seventh or eighth episode.
She reported that she had been experiencing pain, but it had resolved after passing the stone, which she estimated took around 3 days.
She denied any current pain, blood in her urine, or other symptoms.
She also reported a history of hypertension and diabetes, for which she is taking norvasc 2.5mg daily and metformin 500mg daily, respectively.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, diabetes, and recurrent kidney stones.
Review of systems: The patient denied any symptoms in other organs.
Current medications:
Norvasc 2.5mg daily for hypertension
Metformin 500mg daily for diabetes
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure was 150/90.
Physical exam:
Lungs were clear bilaterally
Heart exam revealed a grade 2 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur
Abdominal exam revealed no tenderness to palpation or CVA tenderness.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a history of recurrent kidney stones, hypertension, and diabetes.
Problem list:
Kidney stones: passed; Hypertension; Diabetes; Insomnia; Systolic ejection murmur.
Plan:
I prescribed Percocet as needed for pain management.
I ordered a urinalysis and urine culture to ensure the kidney stone has been fully cleared.
I gave the patient a referral to a urologist to further evaluate her recurrent kidney stones.
I also referred her to a nutritionist to help with dietary management of her hypertension and diabetes.
I prescribed metformin 500mg daily to continue managing her diabetes.
I advised her to take her blood sugars before each meal to monitor her glucose levels.
I prescribed 10mg of melatonin as needed for insomnia.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in 3 months to re-evaluate her kidney stones, hypertension, and diabetes, as well as her insomnia.
